seoTitle: Frogmore vs Frying Pan — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Frogmore and Frying Pan. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Frogmore vs Frying Pan: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Frogmore and Frying Pan.
heading: Frogmore vs Frying Pan — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Frogmore, New South Wales and Frying Pan, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Frying Pan has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Frogmore (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Frogmore or Frying Pan safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Frogmore has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Frying Pan scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Frogmore scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Frying Pan scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Frogmore or Frying Pan?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Frying Pan has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
